title: ulmus campestris var. suberosa
intro: Ulmus campestris var. suberosa, also known as the cork elm, is a deciduous tree species in the family Ulmaceae that is characterized by its distinctive corky bark and broad, oval-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the bark of the cork elm has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems. The bark of the cork elm tree has been found to exhibit antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing, thereby providing numerous health benefits. The cork elm bark can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
